Explanation: The report presents a systematic HPLC analysis workflow using AsLS baseline correction, peak detection, and multi-channel correlation. Strengths include clear methodology description and appropriate use of technical approaches (Gaussian fitting, DBSCAN clustering, SNR filtering). However, several limitations impact the overall quality: (1) The visualization (Figure 1) lacks explicit axis labels and regression lines, limiting precise quantification; (2) Results rely heavily on correlation findings without providing specific numerical metrics (correlation coefficients, R² values); (3) The gap between visual analysis and detailed peak catalog integration remains unresolved; (4) Limited discussion of why outliers represent contaminants or of contamination mechanisms; (5) Code appendix is lengthy but analysis narrative could better synthesize findings. The work demonstrates competent technical execution but lacks the depth of insight expected for distinguishing between target products and contaminants definitively.
